prose_suggestion:
Scope's `cycle_member` indicator is positive, meaning
the scope is in a strongly-connected component of the
import graph (size >= 2). Even a small change to one
member tends to ripple through the cycle. Suggested
refactor: invert one of the dependencies (DIP) so the
edges flow through a single abstraction, OR extract a
shared interface and let both former members depend on
it -- the Refactor Planner emits this as a
`kind='break_cycle'` task (Sec 5.5.3).

Scope's `duplication_ratio` exceeds the seeded
threshold (v1 default: file scope, op=gt, value=0.20 --
more than 20% of LoC sits inside duplicated blocks).
Suggested refactor: extract the duplicated text into a
single canonical implementation and depend on it from
each former duplicate; the Refactor Planner emits this
as a `kind='consolidate_duplication'` task.

public methods, suggesting it handles too many concerns.
Suggested refactor: split the class into focused,
single-responsibility components (SRP).
